manufacturer.comment: Brand owned by Shenzhen Wesion Technology Co., Ltd.

release-date.comment: Public availability September 2022 ($199 launch for Maker Kit Basic).

status.comment: Sold direct from Khadas store and via Amazon US as of 2026-04-26.

open-source-hardware.comment: Khadas publishes hardware reference docs but not full PCB design files under an OSHW-compatible license; per RESEARCH.md guideline this does not qualify.

country-of-origin.comment: Khadas / Shenzhen Wesion Technology Co., Ltd. is headquartered in Shenzhen, China.

soc.comment: RK3588S2 is the same silicon as RK3588S in a refreshed package; Khadas spec page calls out S2.

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: A76 cluster runs up to 2.25 GHz; A55 cluster at 1.8 GHz. Some Khadas docs cite a 2.4 GHz upper bound for the SoC, but the Edge2's published rating is 2.25 GHz.

process-node.comment: RK3588S/S2 fabricated on Samsung 8 nm LPP.

opengl-es-version.comment: Mali-G610 hardware supports OpenGL ES 3.2; mainline Panthor is conformant up to ES 3.1, vendor stack covers ES 3.2.

vulkan-support.comment: Vulkan available via two paths: Rockchip's vendor blob (libmali) and the open-source PanVK driver, which is conformant on Mali-G610.

npu-performance-tops.comment: Vendor-quoted INT8 peak (RK3588S NPU = 6 TOPS aggregate across 3 cores).

hw-video-decode.comment: RK3588S decodes 8K@60 H.265/VP9, 4K@60 AV1, plus H.264. AV1 decode is hardware; AV1 encode is not present.

hw-video-encode.comment: Hardware H.264 and H.265 encode up to 8K@30.

emmc.comment: 32 GB eMMC 5.1 (Basic) or 64 GB eMMC 5.1 (Pro) soldered onboard; no socket.

microsd-slot.comment: No microSD card slot on the board; storage is the soldered eMMC plus USB. Khadas markets the eMMC + OOWOW network installer as the boot path.

m2-nvme-slot.comment: No M.2 connector on the Edge2 itself; the ultraslim form factor leaves no room for M.2.

ethernet.comment: No onboard Ethernet jack. Wired networking requires a USB-C 2.5GbE dongle (CNX review used one).

usb-c-ports-data.comment: Two USB-C ports total: one is USB 3.1 + DP-Alt 1.4 + PD (data-capable), the other is power-only PD. Only the data-capable one counted here per RESEARCH.md.

poe-support.comment: No Ethernet jack on the board, so PoE is not applicable.

max-display-resolution.comment: HDMI 2.1 output up to 8K@60Hz; UI rendering practical limit cited at 4K.

displayport.comment: DisplayPort 1.4 over USB-C DP-Alt (up to 4K@60); no full-size DP connector.

mipi-csi.comment: Three 4-lane MIPI-CSI camera connectors (up to 48 MP each).

audio-jack.comment: No 3.5 mm analog jack. Audio is via HDMI, USB-C, or two onboard digital microphones.

gpio-pins.comment: The Edge2 itself has no through-hole GPIO pin header; signals are exposed only via two 30-pin FPC connectors and 7 pogo pads. A 16-pin GPIO header exists on the optional Edge2 IO carrier board, not on the SBC. Recording null because the schema's 'pin count' field doesn't fit FPC-only exposure.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: No 40-pin RPi-compatible header. I/O is via FPC connectors and pogo pads.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Form factor is 82×57.5×5.7 mm with no RPi-pattern header or mounting holes.

pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: RK3588S has PCIe internally but the Edge2 does not expose PCIe to a user-accessible connector (no M.2, no FPC PCIe). USB 3.1 is the high-speed expansion path.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: I2C, SPI, UART, I2S, ADC and PWM exposed via the two 30-pin FPC connectors (and on the optional Edge2 IO carrier's 16-pin header).

power-input.comment: Powered via the dedicated PD-only USB-C port; Khadas ships a 30 W PD adapter with the kit.

idle-power-w.comment: CNX-Software Edge2 Pro review measured 2.9 W idle on Ubuntu 22.04.

load-power-w.comment: CNX measured 9.0 W during 8-core stress test; 4K video peaked at 7.5–10.9 W.

cooling-required.comment: Khadas ships an active fansink kit and recommends it for sustained load on the RK3588S; review measured 61.9 °C peak with the included cooler.

rtc-battery-header.comment: Onboard 3 V RTC battery / connector with companion STM32 microcontroller.

length-mm.comment: Maker Kit dimensions 82.0 × 57.5 × 5.7 mm; ARM PC enclosure 89.0 × 62.0 × 16.0 mm.

form-factor-class.comment: 82×57.5 mm is close to Pi-standard size but mounting holes and pinout follow Khadas's own slim-SBC pattern; per RESEARCH.md guideline this is `custom`.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: Khadas Edge-series mounting pattern; not Pi-compatible.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: rk3588s-khadas-edge2.dts has been in mainline since Linux 6.x; basic boot (UART, eMMC, USB-C DRD) works upstream. Wi-Fi (AP6275P), NPU, full GPU/video acceleration still need vendor blobs/patches. Armbian ships both a 6.18 mainline track and a 6.1 vendor track.

official-os-image.comment: Khadas publishes maintained Ubuntu 22.04 and Android 11/12 images, distributed via the OOWOW network installer.

distros-with-images.comment: Khadas publishes Ubuntu (and Debian variants); Armbian provides Ubuntu 24.04/26.04 and Debian 13/14 images on both mainline 6.18 and vendor 6.1 kernels.

bootloader.comment: U-Boot (Rockchip vendor fork; mainline U-Boot patch series for the Edge2 has been posted). OOWOW is Khadas's installer running on top of U-Boot.

long-term-support.comment: No vendor-stated EOL window. Khadas continues to publish updates 3.5 years post-launch and Armbian still ships fresh mainline builds; placing this in the 3-5y bucket conservatively.

android-support.comment: Khadas publishes Android 11/12 images via OOWOW.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Maker Kit Basic (8 GB / 32 GB) launch price; checked 2026-04-26. Khadas store still lists the Edge2 family but the live shop page was not directly readable in this research pass — original launch MSRP is the most defensible figure.

msrp-top-usd.comment: ARM PC Pro (16 GB / 64 GB) launch price.

available-regions.comment: Sold direct from Khadas worldwide and via Amazon US; European resellers stock the Edge series.

typical-lead-time.comment: Listed at Khadas store and Amazon as of 2026-04-26.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive Khadas docs portal with hardware reference, FPC pinouts, OOWOW, build guides, and a published specs PDF. Loses a point because the bundled PDF rendered as binary content in this pass and some sub-pages 404.

community-size.comment: Khadas runs a vendor forum and Discord; per RESEARCH.md thresholds Khadas falls into the 'medium' tier alongside Radxa and FriendlyELEC.

active-forum-discord.comment: Vendor-run Khadas forum has a dedicated Edge2 sub-category with regular activity; Khadas also runs an official Discord.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Khadas ships official accessories: Edge2 IO carrier, Edge2 Station dock, active fansink, USB-C PD PSU, antennas, TS050 touchscreen. Smaller than the Pi/Radxa ecosystems and no native HAT story (no 40-pin header).
